A design system bridges the gap between design and development, ensuring consistency across applications. Modern design systems are:
: Integrated workflows from design tools (Figma) to component libraries
: Separation of design tokens from implementation
to host CSS/Sass and design tokens while building a separate Blazor component library that consumes those styles. This hybrid approach combines the best of both worlds:

Figma Design → Storybook (CSS/Design Tokens) ↔ Figma<>Storybook Plugin
                                              ↓
                                    Blazor Component Library
                                              ↓
                                    RCL (Razor Class Library)
                                              ↓
                                    Multiple Projects
allows you to link live stories directly to Figma components, creating a bidirectional design-to-code connection.
demonstrates an alternative approach primarily built with React and TypeScript, supporting React, Vue, and Web Components rather than Blazor.
: Open-source, Material Design-based library. Highly customizable with built-in theming support. Popular for modern enterprise UIs.
. Ensures consistency with modern Microsoft applications.
, a WYSIWYG designer for visual logic management.
